arts.ttl: Arsenal transfer news: Mikel Arteta's side to sign £51m midfielder 'next summer' as Liverpool, Man City suffer crushing blow
arts.sum: Arsenal will reportedly complete the signing of Real Sociedad midfielder Martin Zubimendi at the end of the season.

arts.ttl: Brentford vs Manchester City – Match preview and team news
arts.sum: Manchester City travel to Brentford tonight looking to make it four successive victories in all competitions. Brentford were dumped out of the FA Cup at the weekend courtesy of a stunner from Plymouth’s Morgan Whittaker. Rico Henry started his first game since September 2023 against Argyle after recovering from an anterior cruciate ligament injury. arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola seen still wearing his wedding ring as he looks stressed on first appearance since 'split from wife' emerged, at London hotel before tonight's Man City match
arts.sum: Observers are asking if his relationship troubles could be behind the strange outbursts including a joke about self harming and several furious rows with fans on and off the pitch.

arts.ttl: Brentford have ‘entered the race’ for Crystal Palace target – Only €12m needed for January deal
arts.sum: Brentford have ‘entered the race’ for Benfica’s Jan-Niklas Beste and could rival Crystal Palace for the winger. That’s according to journalist Florian Plettenberg, who reports that Brentford are the latest to show an interest in the 26-year-old. He states that Brentford have joined the list of suitors for Beste, with Borussia Monchengladbach also very interested The post Brentford have ‘entered the race’ for Crystal Palace target – Only €12m needed for January deal appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Tony Book: The late-blooming bricklayer who became a Manchester City great
arts.sum: The full-back did not play top-flight football until his move to City at the age of 32 in 1966.

arts.ttl: Former Man City captain and manager Tony Book dies aged 90
arts.sum: Tony Book led Manchester City to the first division title in 1968 and also lifted the FA Cup in 1969

arts.ttl: Former Manchester City captain and manager Tony Book dies aged 90
arts.sum: Right-back Book captained City as they won four major trophies.

arts.ttl: Player saying goodbye as he heads to Manchester City – Transfer agreed, documents being exchanged
arts.sum: Manchester City’s move for Palmeiras defender Vitor Reis is now in the ‘document exchange phase’. That’s according to journalist André Hernan, who reports that Reis is ‘already saying goodbye’ to his Palmeiras teammates. arts.ttl: Tony Book, Manchester City title winner and ‘club legend’, dies aged 90
arts.sum: Book won trophies as player and manager at City His other clubs as a defender included Plymouth The former Manchester City captain and manager Tony Book has died at the age of 90. Book, whose honours as a City player included a First Division title in 1968 and an FA Cup a year later, also played for clubs including Plymouth. City said in a statement: “It is with huge sadness and the heaviest of hearts that we announce the passing of former Manchester City captain and manager Tony Book, aged 90. A true club legend in every sense of the word, Tony made 315 appearances for City in total between 1966 and 1974, scoring five goals.” Continue reading...
